results.possibleIdentity: Israel David was a mechanical engineer based on Long Island, New York, who was active during the late 1960s and early 1970s. He is historically identified as a volunteer engineer for Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the arts and the scientific/engineering communities. According to E.A.T. records, David was employed by Airborne Instrument Labs (AIL) in Deer Park, Long Island, and lived or maintained a professional presence in Lynbrook, New York. His technical expertise and interests included mathematics, the study of geometric structures, and the design of mechanical devices for curve-designing. His involvement with E.A.T. typically placed him in the organization's Technical Services Program, which matched artists with engineers capable of providing specific technical solutions for creative projects.
